Raymond Simmons, Plaintiff in pro per and moving party, (415-824-2435).

ARGUED briefly by Mr. Simmons. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SIMMONS' Motion For The Court To Correct An Erroneous Ruling On Its Own Motion is DENIED. Prevailing party to prepare and submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2014 Continued To Jun-11-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on March 17, 2014. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
Defendant AMERESCO, INC.'S Demurer to Second Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Taking all allegations in the Second Amend Complaint as true Ameresco's alleged actions do not rise to the requisite level of outrageousness to support a cause of action fro Intentional infliction of Emotional Distress. Also, no facts are alleged showing intent/reckless disregard to cause emotional distress. Prevailing party to submit a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Raymond Simmons, plaintiff in pro per, (415-824-2435) / E. Forest Shryock, Jr., Esq. (415-398-0200) for Ameresco, defendant and opposing party

Plaintiff argues re dismissal of defendant SF Housing Authority only and defendant Ameresco is still a remaining defendant in his complaint. Counsel Shryock responds on behalf of Ameresco. Court reviews the complaint and dismissal papers. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: Plaintiff Raymond B.
